title: The Kitchen Sink
author: MichaelMcCulloch

data.description: Uses relu_squared as the default MLP activation, with optional leaky_relu_squared or star_relu.
Mixed pre-norm/post-norm scheme with post-norm used for deeper layers.
Overrides the MLP hidden dimension to widen the feed-forward network.
Cross-sequence attention applied to the last N layers with KV caching.
Auxiliary multi-token prediction heads are added during training and stripped before export.
Hadamard rotation is applied before quantization to improve error distribution.
Post-TTT temperature scaling is used for calibration.
Multi-order backoff n-gram cache with entropy-adaptive interpolation.
Hidden-state nearest-neighbor language model used at evaluation time.
3-bit KV cache compression via random rotation and scalar quantization.

novel_contributions: A single train_gpt.py combining 26 independently toggleable techniques
Hybrid architecture with ReLU² activation, HybridNorm, widened MLPs, and XSA
Training enhancements including EMA, SWA, QAT, and multi-token prediction heads
Compression/quantization stack with variable bit-width quantization, OptRot, GPTQ, pruning, and entropy coding
Evaluation-time augmentation with LoRA TTT, n-gram cache, kNN-LM, and TurboQuant
Ablation framework for measuring isolated contribution of each technique
